steps.title: Welcome to Week 3 — Real Business Impact
steps.body:
Weeks 1 and 2 gave you the skills to use AI and build systems. Now you learn something different: applying AI to specific business functions — operations, marketing, sales, product, finance, HR.

Today: Business Operations. The engine room of every company.

Operations is where AI delivers the fastest ROI: automating workflows, analyzing data, managing customer support, and optimizing processes. This is where you move from 'AI user' to 'AI-driven operator' of a real business function.

Quickest AI wins in operations:

• Support ticket triage → 70% reduction in first-response time
• Data extraction from documents → 90% faster than manual
• Workflow automation → 40+ hours saved per week per team
• Process documentation → hours becomes minutes

These aren't hypothetical. They're documented results from real companies.

steps.question: A company receives 500 support tickets per week. 60% are password resets, account status checks, and basic how-to questions. What's the highest-leverage AI ops move?
options: Hire 3 more support agents to handle the volume
Build an AI chatbot trained on the FAQ and knowledge base to handle the 60% repetitive tickets
Ignore the ticket pattern — it's not that bad
Send mass emails to customers asking them to stop submitting tickets
feedback: Exactly. The 60% of repetitive tickets are a perfect fit for AI automation. The chatbot handles Tier 0 questions instantly, humans handle the remaining 40% that need judgment. Both response time and team satisfaction improve.

Not quite. Hiring more people for repetitive work scales the problem instead of solving it. The leverage play is AI-handling the predictable 60% so your team focuses on the 40% that needs human expertise.

steps.title: The Ops Transformation — GreenLeaf Retail
steps.body:
GreenLeaf is a mid-size retailer with 12 stores. Before AI ops:
• Weekly inventory reports took 8 hours of manual spreadsheet work
• Customer complaints sat unread for 3+ days
• Employee onboarding paperwork took 2 weeks to process
• Vendor invoices had 15% error rate going unnoticed

After AI ops implementation:
• Inventory reports are auto-generated daily from POS data — 15 minutes
• AI triages customer complaints: urgent issues flagged within 1 hour
• Onboarding: AI reviews documents, generates checklist, sends reminders — 2 days
• Invoice errors detected in real-time by pattern-matching AI — 2% error rate

Inventory savings alone paid for the system in 3 months.

steps.question: AI is most useful in business operations for tasks that are high-volume, repetitive, and follow predictable patterns.
feedback: Correct. High-volume + repetitive + predictable = ideal AI automation territory. These are the tasks that drain human energy and benefit most from consistent, always-on AI processing.
Actually yes. AI excels at exactly these tasks: high frequency, low variability, clear success criteria. Operations is full of them — which is why AI ROI in ops is so fast.

steps.title: Build an Ops Automation — Invoice Data Extraction
steps.prompt:
The setup: You receive 200 PDF invoices per week. Someone manually reads each one and enters vendor, amount, date, PO number, and line items into your accounting system.

The AI solution pipeline:

1. Input: Invoices arrive in a shared email inbox
2. AI layer: Custom GPT or Make scenario reads each PDF, extracts structured fields
3. Validation: AI flags any invoice that doesn't match expected patterns (possible error)
4. Output: Structured data goes into your accounting system via API
5. Exception handling: Flagged invoices get human review (usually 5-10%)

Result: One person reviews 10-20 invoices per day instead of 200. The AI handles 90% perfectly and improves over time.

steps.title: Design an Ops Automation for Your Context
steps.instruction:
Think of one repetitive operational task in your business or work that:
• Happens at least weekly
• Follows a predictable pattern
• Takes someone more than 2 hours per week
• Has clear inputs and outputs

Describe the current process, the AI automation you'd build, and estimate the time saved.
steps.placeholder:
Task: [what's the repetitive process]
Current time: [hours/week]
AI solution: [how would you automate it]
Time saved: [estimated hours/week]
Tools needed: [which no-code/AI tools]

steps.title: Three Ops Automation Categories
steps.body:
Most business ops automations fall into three buckets:

1. DATA PIPELINES — Extract, transform, and load data from one system to another. Examples: invoice processing, report generation, database syncing.

2. TRIAGE SYSTEMS — Route incoming items to the right person or process. Examples: support ticket routing, email classification, lead assignment.

3. MONITORING & ALERTS — Watch for patterns and notify when something needs attention. Examples: inventory below threshold, unusual transaction detected, SLA breach warning.

Pick the category that matches your bottleneck and start there.

steps.title: Process Documentation — The Hidden Time Sink
steps.body:
Maria's team has high turnover. Every time someone leaves, the next person spends weeks figuring out how processes work — tribal knowledge that walks out the door.

AI solution:
• She records a 20-min Loom walking through a process
• Feeds the transcript into a GPT with instructions: 'Convert this into a step-by-step SOP document with screenshots described, common pitfalls, and FAQ'
• The GPT produces a draft SOP in 2 minutes
• Maria reviews and edits — 10 minutes

Result: 12 SOPs created in a single afternoon. Previously: zero, because nobody had time.

Process documentation no longer has to compete with 'real work.' It IS real work — now done at AI speed.

questions.prompt: What operational task in your work is the biggest time drain? Estimate hours per week. What would change if AI handled 80% of it?
questions.placeholder: The task, hours per week, and how your role would change...

questions.prompt: What would you do with the time saved from ops automation? Which higher-value work becomes possible?
questions.placeholder: How you'd reinvest the saved time...

steps.body:
Operations is where AI goes from 'interesting' to 'indispensable.'

When you automate a marketing task, you save marketing time.
When you automate an operations task, you save EVERYONE'S time.

That's the multiplier effect of AI in ops. Don't just automate your job — automate the systems that everyone depends on.
highlights: automate the systems everyone depends on

steps.title: Day 15 Complete!
steps.body:
You now understand the three categories of AI ops automation — data pipelines, triage systems, and monitoring alerts. You've seen real examples of companies cutting hours to minutes on operational tasks.

Tomorrow: AI for Marketing & Content — how to generate, personalize, and distribute content at scale.

You're building a complete AI operator toolkit, one business function at a time.
